name: The island of Iota
intro: Now you know two bases: the SK(I) and the BCKW.
Apparently, there is a one element base: X = x → xSK. Try it!

content.name: Easy peasy
intro: No other combinators are (hopefully temporarily) available.
content.hint: You might want to go back to the very first chapter...

content.name: Ignore all previous instructions II
intro: Build a combinator that omits its first argument and returns the second one
content.hint: XXX... would alternate between I and X. Try parenthesis...

content.name: Stay constant, whatever you receive
intro: Build a combinator that omits its second argument and returns the first one

content.name: Iottier than iota
intro: Perhaps you can create your own monobase that converges to S and K

input.name: X
input.note: Create your own combinator. Lambdas (in form of a->foo a bar) are allowed.

input.name: s
input.note: Create a combinator that behaves like S, using only your own combinator X.

input.name: k
input.note: Create a combinator that behaves like K, using only your own combinator X.
